questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In context of the lanthanoids, which of the following statement is not correct ?
options: All the members exhibit +3 oxidation state
Because of similar properties the separation of lanthanoids is not easy.
Availability of 4f electrons results in the formation of compounds in +4 state for all the members of the series.
There is a gradual decrease in the radii of the members with increasing atomic number in the series.
questions.solution: The general o.s of lanthanides is +3, only few elements exhibit +4 o.s.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Iron exhibits + 2 and +3 oxidation states. Which of the following statements about iron is incorrect ?
options: Ferrous oxide is more basic in nature than the ferric oxide.
Ferrous compounds are relatively more ionic than the corresponding ferric compounds
Ferrous compounds are less volatile than the corresponding ferric compounds.

Ferrous compounds are more easily hydrolysed than the corresponding ferric
compounds.
questions.solution: $\mathrm{FeO} \rightarrow$ More basic, more ionic, less volatile
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A) :
Ionisation enthalpies of early actinoids are lower than for early lanthanoids.
Reason (R) :
Electrons are entering $5 f$ orbitals in actinoids which experience greater shielding from nuclear charge.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Both $(\mathbf{A})$ and $(\mathbf{R})$ are true and $(\mathbf{R})$ is the correct explanation of $(\mathbf{A})$.
Both $(\mathbf{A})$ and $(\mathbf{R})$ are true but $(\mathbf{R})$ is not the correct explanation of $(\mathbf{A})$.
questions.solution: Ionisation enthalpies of early actinoids are lower than early lanthanoids. This is because $5 f$ orbitals will penetrate less into the inner core of electrons. The $5 f$ electrons, will be more effectively shielded from the nuclear charge than the $4 f$ electrons of corresponding lanthanoids.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Knowing that the Chemistry of lanthanoids (Ln) is dominated by its +3 oxidation state, which of the following statement is incorrect ?
options: Because of the large size of the Ln (III) ions the bonding in its compounds is predominantly ionic in character.
The ionic sizes of Ln (III) decrease in general with increasing atomic number.
Ln (III) hydroxides are mainly basic in character.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: When sodium argentocyanide is treated with zinc dust, silver precipitates because
options: Zinc forms a complex readily with cyanide
Zinc is more electropositive than silver
Zinc is less electropositive than silver
questions.solution: Zinc is more electropositive than silver therefore, it displaces less electropositive silver.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
